commit.message:
Merge pull request #26 from dchu3/fix/cli-input-cursor

fix: use useReducer for atomic cursor/value updates and split delete/backspace

Fixes from multi-model review:
- Stale closure: replace separate useState for value+cursor with
  useReducer so all mutations are atomic (no batched-input desync)
- Delete key: split backspace/delete into separate handlers; delete
  removes char at cursor without moving, backspace removes before cursor
- Down arrow: only restore draft when leaving history (index 0→-1),
  ignore when already at -1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix zoxide/delta install to use actual release asset URLs

The .deb filename pattern was wrong for zoxide (expected
zoxide_VERSION_ARCH.deb but actual is zoxide_VERSION-1_ARCH.deb).
Instead of guessing filenames, extract the browser_download_url
directly from the GitHub releases API for both delta and zoxide.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 0 — AI core library (context + reasoner + LLM client)

Shared core at src/lib/ai/ for CLI, MCP, webhook, and agent mode:
- types.ts: SpiderContext, LLMConfig, ReasoningResult, Advice, EventPayload
- llm-client.ts: Gemma 4 (LM Studio) default, local-first fallback
  (lmstudio → ollama → copilot opt-in), auto GitHub token via gh CLI
- context-builder.ts: Aggregates registry (via storage adapter),
  git status, colony snapshot, activity log — all best-effort
- reasoner.ts: Scenario playbook embedded from Spider's Maze,
  3 modes (ask/advise/explain), command extraction from responses
- event-handler.ts: Deterministic tier detection → LLM escalation
  for TIER 2+ only
- index.ts: Barrel export

Tested: TypeScript clean, ping/context/reason all working against
local Gemma 4 26B on LM Studio.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix ev formulas (#52)

* fix: improve EV formulas with one-level lookahead

Check EV now models villain continuation (check-back vs bet) with hero
optimally calling or folding when facing a bet, instead of the simplistic
equity * pot formula that assumed immediate showdown.

Bet/raise EV now allows hero to call villain's raise when profitable,
instead of always assuming hero folds (which made raise_branch = -bet).

Extracted compute_check_ev() and compute_aggressive_ev() as testable
helper functions with 13 unit tests covering edge cases and formula
correctness.

Closes #49

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: compute postflop raise sizes as multiplier of villain's bet

R3x/R4x/R5x were calculated as 300-500% of pot, resulting in
massive overbets (12-20x villain's bet) that gave hero excessive
fold equity and made calling never optimal.

Now computed as 3x/4x/5x the villain's bet amount, producing
realistic raise sizes (e.g. R3x facing a 33% cbet = 74% pot
instead of 300% pot).

Also fixed pot calculation in the router to handle raises
consistently when they appear in action history.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add pot/sizing debug stats to solver output

Shows pot size (BB), call amount (BB), bet size (BB + %pot) for
each action in the debug panel. Helps diagnose solver issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct pot double-counting in raise EV formula

When hero raises over villain's bet, pot_bb already includes the
villain's prior bet (call_amount_bb). The formula was using
pot_bb + 2*bet_bb which double-counts call_amount_bb.

Fixed by subtracting call_amount_bb from both the call branch
and re-raise branch pot totals. Added call_amount_bb parameter
to compute_aggressive_ev() (defaults to 0 for open bets).

Added test verifying correct pot accounting for raise-over-bet
scenario (R5x facing B33: 23.65 BB not 25.465 BB).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add SPR-aware future value to EV calculations

On early streets, the solver now estimates additional EV from remaining
streets when villain commits chips (calls a bet or bets after a check).
This makes smaller bets more attractive for value hands because they
keep villain in for future streets.

Key mechanics:
- Future value = (2*equity - 1) * remaining_stack * extraction_rate * spr_factor
- Higher SPR (smaller bets) -> more room for future betting -> higher bonus
- Only hands with equity > 50% get the bonus (floored at 0)
- Applied to: bet/call branch, raise/hero-calls branch, check/facing-bet branch
- NOT applied to: fold branches, check-back branch (no committed money)

Example impact (AK on K72r, 100 BB deep):
- Before: R5x (9.57) > B33 (7.48) -- solver always raises big
- After:  B33 (11.22) > R5x (10.72) -- solver prefers smaller bet

Also adds effective stack and SPR to debug panel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* tune: reduce extraction rate and steepen SPR curve

Lower EXTRACTION_RATE from 0.15 to 0.10 to prevent over-slowplaying.
Switch SPR factor from linear (spr/10) to sqrt curve (sqrt(spr/8))
which more steeply penalises low-SPR situations where hero is
pot-committed and has minimal optionality on future streets.

Effect: B33 ≈ R5x ≈ B66 for value hands (~13 BB each), producing
a realistic mixed strategy instead of always slowplaying.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use hard splits for sub-range equity calculations

eval7 ignores combo weights, so the dampening factor (0.3) had no
effect on equity calculations — eq_vs_callers was identical to
eq_vs_full_range. This caused the solver to overbet with marginal
hands because it thought QQ's 63% equity held against callers too.

Added hard_split parameter to filter_range_by_equity(). When True,
combos only appear in their assigned bucket (for equity calc).
When False (default), soft dampening is preserved for cross-street
range narrowing so hands don't drop out too quickly.

EV calculation call sites now use hard_split=True. Range narrowing
in compute_current_ranges() keeps soft splits (hard_split=False).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deduplicate env keys in reusable release workflow (#956)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

placed outside the ThemeProvider tree.

Components are no-ops in development; data is only collected when
deployed to Vercel with Analytics and Speed Insights enabled in the
dashboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve latest issue.md backlog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stable_diffusion): implement SDCppGenerator, real PNG encoder, and SDStubGenerator img2img pass-through (#4576)

- Add SDCppGenerator (sd_generator.h): wraps stable-diffusion.cpp txt2img/img2img
  C API; compiled only when THEMIS_ENABLE_STABLE_DIFFUSION is defined; SDPlugin
  default ctor uses it automatically when the lib is available.
- Fix encodeMinimalPng (sd_plugin.cpp): replace stub (IHDR+IEND, no IDAT, zero CRC)
  with a self-contained encoder using stored-deflate zlib, CRC-32, and Adler-32.
  No external deps required.
- Add SDStubGenerator::generateImg2Img override (sd_generator.h): returns input
  image data as output (pass-through) when valid input is provided; falls back to
  generate() otherwise.
- Add 6 new tests (groups P and Q, 51 total): PNG signature/IDAT/IHDR checks and
  SDStubGenerator img2img dimension/data/fallback verification.
- Update ROADMAP.md: mark completed items, update test count to 51.

commit.message:
Replace AmidiTransport with persistent DryWetMidi connection

- Implement DryWetMidiConnection with persistent input listener,
  address-keyed TCS reply correlator, and PushNotificationReceived event
- Switch DefaultMidiTransport to always use DryWetMidiTransport on all platforms
- Add PushNotificationReceived to IMidiConnection and IKatanaSession
- KatanaSession forwards push events and writes EDITOR_COMMUNICATION_MODE=1
  on connect (tells amp to send unsolicited DT1 push notifications) and 0 on disconnect
- MainWindowViewModel subscribes to push notifications, builds an address-to-action
  lookup at connect time, and applies push values via ApplyDeviceState to update
  the UI without echo-writing back to the amp
- Document BTS architecture, reply correlator, and state update pattern in TECHNICAL_NOTES.md

Each RQ1→DT1 round-trip now takes the actual device latency (~100-200ms)
instead of the amidi recording duration (2s). Full initial load drops from ~30s to ~2-3s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Guard: no config → always redirect to federation-setup

Three layers of protection:
1. SessionStart hook: checks federate.config.json first. If missing, ALL
   federation requests route to setup wizard.
2. federation-orchestration skill: prerequisites section redirects to setup.
3. @federation agent: first instruction is check config, redirect if missing.

No matter which entry point triggers — hook, skill, or agent — a missing
config always lands on the setup wizard.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

using third-party actions.# Summary

commit.message:
Fix skill trigger overlap — setup vs orchestration

federation-setup: triggers on creation intent (go multi-team, I need teams,
federate this project). Explicitly notes: only when no config exists yet.

federation-orchestration: triggers on management intent (launch team,
monitor, send directive, sync skills). Only when config already exists.

Removed vague 'federate' and 'federation' triggers from orchestration
that were catching setup-intent phrases.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: polish UX flows and stabilize e2e coverage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update dashboard counts and show success feedback after group dismiss (#73)

* fix: update dashboard counts and show success feedback after group dismiss

- Add onDismissed(count) callback to NotificationList so it can
  propagate dismissals up to DashboardPanel
- Thread callback through RepoHealthRow via onNotifsDismissed prop
- In DashboardPanel.handleNotifsDismissed: decrement notificationCount
  and totalNotifications, strip the 'has-notifications' warning when
  count reaches 0, and recompute reposWithWarnings — so all badges
  (337 Notifications card, per-repo pill, section header) update
  immediately without a full refresh
- Show a transient '✓ N notifications dismissed' success banner
  inside NotificationList that auto-clears after 3 seconds
- Success banner also appears on the 'No notifications' empty state so
  the user sees confirmation even after the last notification clears

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent dashboard from expanding beyond viewport width

- Add overflow-x: hidden to .dashboard-panel so notification group
  headers (which use white-space: nowrap) cannot force the panel wider
  than the viewport
- Add min-width: 0 to .dash-notif-group-name so the flex ellipsis
  actually kicks in when the header is constrained

When the Notifications card is active, show Count / Name toggle buttons

commit.message:
fix: add cursor movement and fix display jumping in CLI input

- Add cursor position tracking with left/right arrow key support
- Insert and delete at cursor position instead of only at end
- Add Ctrl+A (Home), Ctrl+E (End), Ctrl+U (clear line) shortcuts
- Render inverse cursor character at current position
- Save draft text when navigating command history
- Fix display jumping with fixed-height input container

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: phone-gated mode — LINE_FOLLOW when no phone, COMMAND when paired

- Robot boots into LINE_FOLLOW mode (Arduino handles autonomously)
- When tourist scans QR and phone pairs → switches to COMMAND/AI mode
- When phone disconnects or trip ends → reverts to LINE_FOLLOW
- Dashboard shows 'Line Mode' badge and info banner when no phone
- Control buttons disabled in LINE_FOLLOW mode
- API robot-state now includes mode, safety, phone_paired fields

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add test-coverage-improver agentic workflow (#13)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ALTrijssenaar/copilot-adventures-ALTrijssenaar/sessions/506b5259-4deb-4f2d-b765-88235a092846

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ALTrijssenaar <6206388+ALTrijssenaar@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve dotfiles: security, CI, deps, and code quality

Security & hardening:
- Harden SSH config (IdentitiesOnly, ServerAlive, ControlMaster)
- Modernize GPG keyservers (defunct SKS pool -> keys.openpgp.org)
- Add git transfer/fetch/receive fsckObjects for integrity checks
- Replace pipe-to-bash zoxide install with direct .deb download

CI/CD:
- Remove commented-out Safari macOS defaults (dead code)

Reproducibility:
- Pin Ansible role/collection versions in requirements.yml
- Pin Ruby gem versions in Gemfile
- Pin Python package versions in requirements.txt

Code quality:
- Remove debug flags (set -ex -> set -e) from setup/clean scripts
- Add lib/* to shellcheck and shfmt linting
- Improve install.sh: backup non-symlink files, warn on chsh failure
- Use yq instead of fragile shell parsing in config tests

commit.message:
fix(service): use replaceView for seamless background/foreground video

Previously, backgrounding the app triggered surfaceDestroyed() which
called stopPreview(), killing Camera2 capture. On return, startPreview()
created a new RtmpCamera2/SrtCamera2 instance, orphaning the old one
with the active connection. Result: frozen video, audio continued.

Now StreamingService uses replaceView during active streams:
- detachPreviewSurface(): calls replaceViewWithBackground() to switch
  to headless mode (camera keeps capturing, encoder stays connected)
- attachPreviewSurface(): calls replaceView(openGlView) to hot-swap
  back to the new surface without interrupting the stream

stopPreview() is still used when no stream is active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: StreamCaster-android
repository.description: StreamCaster is a native Android app for live streaming camera and/or microphone to RTMP/RTMPS endpoints.

commit.message:
feat(service): add replaceView methods to EncoderBridge for background streaming

Add replaceViewWithBackground(Context) and replaceView(OpenGlView) to
the EncoderBridge interface, wrapping RootEncoder's Camera2Base.replaceView()
API. This enables seamless background/foreground transitions:

- replaceViewWithBackground(): switches to headless GlStreamInterface,
  camera keeps capturing and encoder stays connected
- replaceView(OpenGlView): hot-swaps back to a visible surface mid-stream

Implemented in RtmpCamera2Bridge, SrtCamera2Bridge, and StubEncoderBridge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  sourced from LanguageSystemDefault resource via LocalizationService
- LocalizationService: fix unsafe (string) cast in Initialize() with
  safe 'raw is string s' pattern; validate saved tag against known tags
  before applying PrimaryLanguageOverride; build SupportedLanguages with
  localized system-default display name after setting the override

  removed LanguageOption.SystemDefault

  in code-behind; add AppTitle resource to en-US and de-DE

  match NavConfig.Content

fix: flip playlist dropdown to open downward when near top of screen

Measures button's getBoundingClientRect().top on open; if < 200px from
top of viewport the menu uses top: calc(100% + 8px) instead of
bottom: calc(100% + 8px), preventing it from being hidden behind the
search bar on top search result rows.

Adds test covering the atpb-menu-down class is applied when button is
near top of screen.

fix(multiplayer): fix lobby presence and start game navigation (Epic 18)

Story 18.1: buildConnectedPresence() now constructs LobbyPresence entries
for players not in the initial snapshot instead of discarding them. Known
players still use DB snapshot values; unknown players use self-reported
Presence payload values and sort after known players. Add full unit test
suite for presence.ts.

Story 18.2: handleStartGame() calls router.push() directly after the
game_started broadcast so the host no longer depends on a self-broadcast
round-trip. startGame() in hostActions.ts now only revalidates /play,
removing the harmful lobby-path revalidation that raced against broadcast
navigation.

commit.message:
Redesign remaining views with CSS modules and design system components

- Remove all T.* inline styles and theme.js imports from 11 views
- Create .module.css files using CSS variable design system for each view
- Use Table component for list views (Finance, Sales, Ops, Audit, Knowledge)
- Use Tabs component to replace manual tab implementations (Finance, Sales, Ops)
- Use Stat component for metric grids (Finance, Sales, Ops, Inventory, ROI)
- Use StatusDot component for invoice/task status indicators
- Use Skeleton for loading states in ApprovalView
- Redesign Setup.jsx as polished multi-step wizard with progress dots and industry grid
- Redesign SettingsView with organized sections and integration grid
- Preserve all state, API calls, handlers, and logic exactly

fix(ci): resolve jq Infinity parse error — 5-point patch

Root cause: src/ had 50 files -> CALIBRATION_MILESTONE(=10) multiple
-> _check_calibration_hint() printed to stdout AFTER JSON output
-> jq parsed '{...json...}\n[*] Calibration milestone...' and failed

Fixes:
1. cli_commands.py: _check_calibration_hint() all print() -> file=sys.stderr
2. metrics/inflation.py: float('inf') -> 10.0 (max cap, RFC 8259 safe)
3. core.py: avg_inflation filter: status != 'error' -> math.isfinite()
4. cli.py: _sanitize_for_json() handles tuple; json.dumps(allow_nan=False)
5. tests/e2e_v321: milestone_fired/auto_calibrated check stdout+stderr

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add montage order toggle for song lists (#34)

Adds a button to reorder songs by their montage sequence (alphabetical
by country, matching the ESC montage video) instead of the default
semi-final running order.

### Changes

- **`constants.ts`**: Added `sortSongsByMontageOrder(songs, year)` and
`hasMontageData(year)` helpers that sort songs by `ESC_MONTAGE_DATA`
timestamp order
- **`vote-song-list.tsx`**: Toggle button switches between "📋 Semi-final
order" / "🎬 Montage order"; hides semifinal group headers in montage
mode
- **`dashboard-controls.tsx`**: Same toggle; `handleSkip` now follows
the active display order
- **`vote.tsx`**: Passes `escYear` to `VoteSongList`

The toggle only renders when montage data exists for the game's ESC
year.

---------

commit.message:
Archetype setup wizard handoff pattern

Core federation-setup rewritten from scratch (v0.2.0):
- Stripped all archetype-specific config (deliverable, schema, steps, roles)
- Core config reduced to 4 fields: description, branchPrefix, mcpStack, telemetry
- Uses 'team' language, not 'domain'
- No upfront team listing — teams onboard one at a time
- After archetype install, hands off to archetype's setup skill

New archetype setup skills:
- deliverable-setup: filename, schema, playbook steps, import hook
- coding-setup: PR target branch, playbook steps, code conventions, test requirements

Both write to .squad/archetype-config.json (team worktree), not core config.
Handoff: core setup → install archetype → archetype setup activates.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove selectedBlock.fg color overrides from the detail panel so

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove @media dark override that bled dark vars into light mode

The @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) block in :root was overriding CSS
variables to dark values even when the user had manually selected light mode
via the toggle (localStorage = '0'). Because the html.dark class was absent,
element-level dark overrides did not apply — but the dark :root vars did.
Result: dark backgrounds/text in light mode for OS-dark-mode users.

Fix: remove the CSS media query block entirely. Theme is now controlled
exclusively by the JS class toggle (html.dark) which already reads OS
preference via matchMedia in initDarkMode(). No behaviour change for
first-time visitors; manual overrides are now respected correctly.

Bonus fix: toggleDarkMode() was replacing btn.textContent with emoji text,
wiping the Lucide <i> icon node. Extracted _darkBtnSync() helper that
updates only the <span> text and swaps the data-lucide attribute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

application messages) had no handler configured and were silently dropped.
Now all Python logging goes to stdout → systemd journal.

fix(firmware): propagate update status from /api/health every 5 s

Previously CanteraFirmwareUpdateEntity only polled /api/update once per
hour, so the sensor could show 'Not Checked' for up to an hour after
restart even when the Pi had a fresh check result.

Now _poll_health() (5-second interval) reads the new firmware_update_status
field from /api/health and calls set_firmware_update_state() immediately,
so the sensor updates within 5 seconds of the Pi's background check.

Also bumps MIN_API_VERSION_MINOR to 2 to match the Pi-side API_VERSION_MINOR
bump for this additive health field.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
